leadText: Shadow Claw (Japanese: シャドークロー Shadow Claw) is a damage-dealing Ghost-type move introduced in Generation IV. It was TM65 from Generation IV through Pokémon Ultra Sun and Ultra Moon and in Generation VIII. In Generation IX, it is TM061.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation VIII 1.1.1 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.2 Generation IX 1.2.1 Pokémon Legends: Z-A Effect [ edit | edit source ] Shadow Claw deals damage and has an increased critical hit ratio. Shadow Claw can also be used as part of a Contest Spectacular combination , with the user gaining extra three appeal points if the move Focus Energy was used in the prior turn. sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description D P Pt HG SS PBR The user slashes with a sharp claw made from shadows. It has a high critical-hit ratio. B W B2 W2 X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP The user slashes with a sharp claw made from shadows. Critical hits land more easily. LA The user slashes with a sharp claw made from shadows. This move has a heightened chance of landing a critical hit. S V The user attacks by slashing the target with a sharp claw made from shadows. This move has a heightened chance of landing a critical hit. ZA The user attacks by slashing with a sharp claw made from shadows. This move has a heightened chance of landing a critical hit.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD T D S Inflicts damage on the target. It has a high critical-hit rate. B S L てきポケモンに ダメージをあたえる きゅうしょに あたりやすい MD GtI S MD MD RT DX It damages an enemy. It has a high critical-hit rate.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Shadow Claw is the only Ghost-type Cute move. Shadow Claw is the only move that retained its TM number between Generation VII and Generation VIII .
